rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic 1,2-diazidation of alkenes mediated by a second-sphere H-bonding manganese amidoporphyrin complex using NaN3 as the azide source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of [Mn(L1)N3] (and/or free N3-) generates an azidyl radical and a higher-valent diazidomanganese(IV) species that delivers the second azide; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: Carbon-centered β-azido alkyl radical (II) after N3• addition to alkene

electron_flow: [Mn(L1)Cl] undergoes ligand exchange with N3- to form [Mn(III)(L1)N3], which is anodically oxidized (and/or free N3- is oxidized) to release an azidyl radical that adds to the alkene; the resulting C-radical then abstracts a second azide via azidyl transfer from the anodically generated [Mn(IV)(L1)(N3)2] to deliver the 1,2-diazide, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: Tetrabutylammonium azide (TBAN3)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation wave at Ep/2 = +453 mV vs Fc+/0; mixing with [Mn(L1)Cl] gave a new redox peak at E1/2 = +155 mV assigned to [Mn(L1)N3], showing ligand exchange lowers MnIII oxidation potential by >600 mV

probe_reagents.name: [Mn(L1)Cl] complex
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows two overlapping one-electron oxidations above +600 mV; UV-vis titration with NaN3 confirms quantitative formation of [Mn(L1)N3]

probe_reagents.name: DFT model [Mn(L3)N3] and [Mn(L3)(N3)2]
probe_reagents.observation: B3LYP-D3(BJ)/def2-SVP calculations identify two N-H···N H-bonds between amide groups and Mn-bound azide (QTAIM BCPs); supports second-sphere stabilization of key intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Chiral D2-symmetric amidoporphyrin Mn catalysts [Mn(L4)Cl] and [Mn(L5)Cl]
probe_reagents.observation: [Mn(L5)Cl] gave 13% ee in diazidation of 4-tert-butylstyrene, implying second azidyl transfer occurs from the chiral Mn azide complex rather than via free diffusion

notes: Authors consider two parallel initiation pathways leading to the same azidyl radical: (i) anodic oxidation of [Mn(L1)N3] to [Mn(L1)(N3)2] followed by homolytic fragmentation, and (ii) direct anodic oxidation of free N3- (both feasible since anode potential reaches +420 mV vs Fc+/0). The second C-N bond is proposed to form by azidyl group transfer from [Mn(IV)(L1)(N3)2] to the transient carbon radical rather than by radical-radical coupling, which is statistically disfavored. Overall cycle formulated as MnIII/MnIV. Second-sphere amide N-H···N3 H-bonds are invoked to stabilize Mn-azide intermediates and may reduce N3 nucleophilicity (no SN2/E2 side products with tosylate substrate 13).
